If the given silicate has $5(\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{Si})$ linkages. Then the value of $(x+y+z)$ is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Coordination number of underlined or mentioned atom is greater than or equal to 4 in how many of the following?
(i) $\mathrm{Na}_2 \underline{\mathrm{SiF}}_6$
(ii) Silicon in Silica
(iii) $\mathrm{CaSiO}_3$ (where the silicate unit is cyclic trisilicate)
(iv) Silicon
(v) $\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\right)_2\right]^{3-}$
(vi) $\mathrm{SO}_3{ }^{2-}$
(vii) $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3$
(viii) $\underline{\mathrm{N}}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$
(ix) $\underline{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$
questions.solution:
(i) 6
Hence the coordination number is 4 . (iv) Silicon $\rightarrow$ Each Si is bonded to four silicon atoms. (v) Coordination Number is 2
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: The total number of lone pairs and the total number of $\sigma$ bonds in $\left[\mathrm{B}_3 \mathrm{O}_3(\mathrm{OH})_6\right]^{3-}$ molecular ion are x and y respectively. What is the value of $x+y$ ?
questions.solution:
Answer (36) The structure of $\left[\mathrm{B}_3 \mathrm{O}_3(\mathrm{OH})_6\right]^{3-}$ is shown below
$x=18, y=18 ; x+y=36$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In case of a boron molecule $\left(B_{12}\right)$, number of faces plus number of corners minus number of edges is
questions.solution:
Number of faces of $B_{12}=20$
Number of corners of $B_{12}=12$
Number of edges of $\mathrm{B}_{12}=30$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
How many statements are true in the given question of about? (i) orthoboric acid It is a strong tribasic acid. (ii) orthoboric acid does not act as a proton donor but behaves like a Lewis acid by accepting $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$ ion. (iii) orthoboric acid is prepared by the action of mineral acid on borax solution. (iv) orthoboric acid has a layer structure in which hydrogen bonds unite $\mathrm{B}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ units. (v) If the number of revolutions made by electron in 1.0 s in H atom in its $\mathrm{n}^{\text {th }}$ orbit is twice of the number of revolution made by electron in 1.0 s in the $2^{\text {nd }}$ orbit of H -atom, then n is 1. (vi) Forms a colourless solution with coloured precipitate(r) $\mathrm{CrCl}_3(\mathrm{~s})+$ dil $\mathrm{HNO}_3(\mathrm{aq})$ (vii) Forms a coloured solution with no precipitate(s) $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2(\mathrm{aq})$ (viii) $\mathrm{F}\gt\mathrm{N}\gt\mathrm{C}\gt\mathrm{Si}\gt\mathrm{Ca}-$ metallic character (ix) Saccharin $\left(\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}=2\right)$ is an artificial zero calorie sweetner added to toothpaste
Sodium Saccharin Structure .Two flasks A\&B both contain 18.3 gm of Saccharin dissolved in 100 ml solution. 2 grams of solid NaOH is added to A and 4 grams of solid NaOH is added to B . This resulted in the formation of the above structure in both flasks. The difference in pH of the two flasks after the experiment is: 6 (x) $\mathrm{C}\gt\mathrm{Si}\gt\mathrm{P}\gt\mathrm{N}$ - electron affinity (xi) $\mathrm{O}\gt\mathrm{N}\gt\mathrm{F}\gt\mathrm{C}$ - second ionisation potential (xii) $\mathrm{I}_2 \lt \mathrm{F}_2 \lt \mathrm{Br}_2 \lt \mathrm{Cl}_2$ Bond energy ORDER Sodium Saccharin Structure .Two flasks A\&B both contain 18.3 gm of Saccharin dissolved in 100 ml solution. 2 grams of solid NaOH is added to A and 4 grams of solid NaOH is added to B . This resulted in the formation of the above structure in both flasks. The difference in pH of the two flasks after the experiment is: 6 (x) $\mathrm{C}\gt\mathrm{Si}\gt\mathrm{P}\gt\mathrm{N}$ - electron affinity (xi) $\mathrm{O}\gt\mathrm{N}\gt\mathrm{F}\gt\mathrm{C}$ - second ionisation potential (xii) $\mathrm{I}_2 \lt \mathrm{F}_2 \lt \mathrm{Br}_2 \lt \mathrm{Cl}_2$ Bond energy ORDER (xiii) mixture of HCl and $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}$ titrated against NaOH the correct graphical representation is
(xiv) The CORRECT ORDER OF basic character of the following alcohols is
questions.solution:
FTTTTFFFTTFTTT
(IX) A is a buffer solution; pH of $\mathrm{A}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}=2$

B is an aqueous solution of $\mathrm{SacNa} ; \mathrm{pH}$ of $\mathrm{B}=\frac{1}{2}\left(\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{w}}+\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \mathrm{c}\right)=8$

questions.q: In the structure of Buckminster's fullerene the number of 5 membered rings and 6 membered rings which are attached to a 6 membered ring are ' $x$ ' and ' $y$ ' respectively. The value of $5(x-y)$ is
questions.solution:
Answer (00)
There are 3 pentagons and 3 hexagons attached to a hexagon in $\mathrm{C}_{60}$
$\therefore \quad x=y=3$
